text.title: Django Celery: Real-World Examples and Agent Gotchas
text.summary: Production-grade examples for common task types plus the most frequent LLM mistakes.

section.title: External API with Rate Limit Handling

example.code: @shared_task( name="integrations.call_payment_gateway", bind=True, max_retries=3, autoretry_for=(requests.exceptions.Timeout, requests.exceptions.ConnectionError), retry_backoff=True, rate_limit="10/m", soft_time_limit=30, time_limit=45, ) def call_payment_gateway(self, payment_id: int, action: str) -> dict: response = requests.post( f"{settings.PAYMENT_GATEWAY_URL}/{action}", json={"payment_id": payment_id}, headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {settings.PAYMENT_API_KEY}"}, timeout=25, ) if response.status_code == 429: raise self.retry(countdown=int(response.headers.get("Retry-After", 60))) response.raise_for_status() return {"status": "success", "data": response.json()}

antipattern.description: LLMs trained on pre-5.4 docs emit transaction.on_commit(lambda: task.delay(...)) boilerplate instead of task.delay_on_commit(...).
antipattern.reason: Both work, but delay_on_commit is cleaner. Standardise on the modern form. Document which version to use in the project AGENTS.md.

antipattern.description: Agents add bind=True to a task but never call self.retry, silently losing retry capability.
antipattern.reason: bind=True is required for self.retry calls and self.request introspection. If neither is needed, omit bind=True.

antipattern.description: Celery Beat in file-based scheduler mode resets schedule on every container redeploy; some periodic tasks miss their slot.
antipattern.reason: Use django-celery-beat (DatabaseScheduler) or celery-redbeat so schedules survive deploys.

antipattern.description: Agents use CELERY_TASK_ALWAYS_EAGER=True in tests and ship code that only works in eager mode.
antipattern.reason: Eager mode hides broker bugs (visibility timeout, serialiser mismatch). Add at least one integration test against a real Redis worker.

antipattern.description: Flower is wired behind a public ingress without authentication, exposing task payloads.
antipattern.reason: Always require basic-auth or VPN-only access for Flower. Task payloads may contain PII or credentials.
